action.reasoning: Observation: predict.py and test_preprocess.py both have syntax errors (truncated predict + mismatched parens in variants list) causing command failure; all prior evals at 0.0 success because of persistent ObservationProcessorStep error (language step mutates dict, drops 'observation' key for subsequent steps) leading to fallback random actions. Mock evaluator reaches 'success' but real rollouts fail. PI0 loads and RecordingEvaluator structure is close. Analysis: Root cause confirmed in diagnostics (pipeline expects transition dict with preserved 'observation' after LanguageProcessorStep; training-oriented processors require dummy 'action' + specific nesting). Step-by-step processor application with deepcopy+merge after language step will fix per test history. This stays on intended Pi0 track (pretrained on similar LIBERO manipulation, good for add_condiment/select tasks). Complete non-truncated predict.py with _prepare_batch (PIL 224x224 views from 4-cam rgb, state pad to 32, dummy action), step-by-step preprocess with observation restore, action chunking, tuned delta bridge (pos*0.05, euler*0.25, grip>0.5), full main() + RecordingEvaluator (super init + evaluate_single_episode per contract, exact JSON episodes). Fixed test_preprocess with clean syntax, step-by-step + merge diagnostic on 6 variants. Decision: Write both files cleanly, run diagnostic+mock test for validation (expect FULL PREPROCESS SUCCEEDED + real policy actions in mock), then submit_eval on base checkpoint for first non-zero success_rate. Measure time; LoRA/finetune in later iters if needed.
